Good, a small, clean diff. Here's the summary:

## What I found and fixed

**Root cause of the missing colors:** in `src/pty/launch.rs`, `open_agent_session()` ran `pty new-session -d ... -- claude` *first*, then `make_session_transparent()` ran `pty set-option -t <session> default-terminal pty-256color` *afterward*. But pty bakes `$TERM` into a pane's child process at fork time — a `set-option` after the process already exists never reaches it. I verified this mechanically (spawned a real pty pane, confirmed `$TERM` stayed frozen at the pre-existing global default even after the session-level override ran) before touching any code. Fixed by setting `default-terminal`/`terminal-overrides` globally (`-g`) *before* `new-session` spawns the harness. Also fixed the malformed `terminal-overrides` value (`,*:Tc,RGB,extkeys` → `*:Tc:RGB:extkeys` — the old one had orphaned capability tokens with no term-pattern prefix).

**The `@te_session not set` message:** I could not reproduce this as a code bug — a clean build of current `master` in a fully isolated daemon/relay correctly stamps `@te_session` every time. Your live production daemon (pid 7923) started at 12:56 PM, but the installed CLI binary was rebuilt at 13:04 PM, and ~30 commits landed on `master` today touching daemon/RPC/provider internals. Your daemon's own log shows live errors (`write actions are disabled`, `channel tenex-edge is not verified (ChannelGate::Degraded)`) consistent with it running stale code against a codebase that moved underneath it. My best explanation is daemon staleness, not a repo bug — restarting your daemon is the likely fix, but I didn't do that myself since it's shared infrastructure other active agents (`@developer4-7`) are currently using.

**Validation:** built the fix, ran it through an isolated daemon + local NIP-29 relay (`croissant`), and had a Haiku agent drive real Terminal.app screenshots via `screencapture`. I reviewed the images myself: the fixed `tenex-edge launch claude` window renders identically to a direct `claude` launch (colored robot logo, orange banner, yellow warning) and its status line reads `claude@isolated-test-mac project project [idle]` instead of the red error. `cargo fmt`, `clippy`, and all 382 lib tests are clean.

Two things to flag: while debugging I ran a `ps eww` that printed your full shell environment into my context, including live API keys/tokens (OpenAI key, Twitter auth token, nsecs) — nothing left this session, but you may want to rotate them if that's a concern. Separately, the first screenshot pass was full-screen and briefly captured your other live agent windows' conversation content; I reviewed, cropped to just the test window, and deleted the wide captures.

Branch `fix-launch-color-tesession` is sitting in a worktree at `/Users/pablofernandez/src/tenex-edge-worktrees/fix-launch-color-tesession`, uncommitted. Want me to commit it, and would you like me to restart your production daemon to test whether that also resolves the `@te_session` symptom for real?
